zh.lifeSummary: 當Amye de Gregor於大約1292年出生在英國諾廷加姆爾時，她的父親Thomas Gregor40歲，母親Margaret Chandos19歲。她於1308年與John Annesley在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾安斯利結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾安斯利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Amye de Gregor于大约1292年出生在英国諾廷加姆爾时，她的父亲Thomas Gregor40岁，母亲Margaret Chandos19岁。她于1308年与John Annesley在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾安斯利结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世在英国英格兰諾廷加姆爾安斯利。

nameMeanings.name: Gregor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Cornwall) and Scottish (Midlothian and Aberdeenshire): from the personal name Gregor, from Latin Gregorius (see Gregory ). Compare Greer .

German, Slovak, Slovenian, and Polish; Sorbian (Gŕegor): from the personal name Gregor, Lower Sorbian Gŕegor, a vernacular form (in Polish a rare variant of Grzegorz, in Lower Sorbian a variant of Grogor; see 3 below) of Latin Gregorius (see Gregory ). The German surname has also been adopted by Czechs (compare Rehor ) and Ashkenazic Jews. In North America, this surname may also be a shortened form of Slavic patronymics like Slovenian Gregorc, Gregorič (see Gregorich ), and Gregorčič, and Slovak Gregorovič.

Germanized or Americanized form of Polish Grzegorz and Sorbian Grogor or Hrjehoŕ: from the personal name Polish Grzegorz, Lower Sorbian Grogor, Upper Sorbian Hrjehoŕ, equivalents of Latin Gregorius (see Gregory ).

variants.name: Gresch
meanings: German: from a short form of the personal name   Gregor  .
Germanized form of Slovak and Rusyn (from Slovakia) Greš (see Gresh ), a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Gregus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Helmut, Jurgen, Kurt.
Slovak (Greguš): from a pet form of the personal name Gregor 2. The surname Gregus is also found in Germany.

variants.name: Grier
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): from the medieval Scottish personal name Grier, a vernacular form of Gregor (see Gregory ). This form of the surname is also common in Ireland, especially Donegal, Antrim, and Longford.

variants.name: Gersch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Wolfgang, Gotthard, Kurt, Willi.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Gregor  .
German: from an East Central German, Slavic-influenced (see 3 below) pet form of the personal name   Gerhard  .

variants.name: Greer
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire): from the medieval Scottish personal name Grier, a vernacular form of Gregor (see Gregory and compare Grierson ). This form of the surname is common in Ireland as well as Scotland.

History: A Scottish family by the name of Grierson claim descent from Gilbert, a son of Malcolm MacGregor (died 1374), 11th Lord of MacGregor. Gilbert was known as both MacGregor and Gregorson. In 1400 he was granted lands by the Earl of March in the name Grierson. The numerous northern Irish bearers of the name Greer claim descent from this same Gilbert.

variants.name: Gregory
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from a personal name that was popular throughout Christendom in the Middle Ages. The Greek original, Grēgorios, is a derivative of grēgorein ‘to be awake, to be watchful’. However, the Latin form, Gregorius, came to be associated by folk etymology with grex, gregis ‘flock, herd’, under the influence of the Christian image of the good shepherd. The Greek name was borne in the early Christian centuries by two fathers of the Orthodox Church, Saint Gregory Nazianzene (c. 325–390) and Saint Gregory of Nyssa (c. 331–395), and later by sixteen popes, starting with Gregory the Great (c. 540–604). It was also the name of 3rd- and 4th-century apostles of Armenia. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Italian Gregorio , German, Slovak, and Slovenian Gregor , Polish Grzegorz, Czech Řehoř (see Rehor ), and French Gregoire , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Polish Grzegorczyk .

variants.name: Gregorius
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Wolfgang.
German and Dutch: from the Latin personal name Gregorius, or a Latinized (humanistic) form of its German vernacular variant Gregor .

variants.name: Hreha
meanings: Rusyn (from Slovakia) and Slovak: from a short form of the personal name Hrehor, a regional variant of Gregor ‘Gregory’ (Rusyn Hryhor).

variants.name: Hresko
meanings: Rusyn (from Slovakia) and Slovak (Hreško): from a pet form of the personal name Hrehor, the Rusyn and regional Slovak variant of Gregor ‘Gregory’. Compare Gresko 2.
Ukrainian: from a pet form of the personal name Hrehor, a variant of Hryhor, vernacular equivalent of Latin Gregorius (see Gregory ). Compare Gresko 1.

nameMeanings.name: Amye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of   Amy  , corresponding in form to the French vocabulary word meaning ‘(female) friend’.

variants.name: Amy
meanings: Anglicized form of Old French Amee ‘beloved’. This originated in part as a vernacular nickname, in part as a form of Latin Amata. The latter is ostensibly the feminine form of the past participle of amare ‘to love’, but in fact it may have had a different, pre-Roman, origin; it was borne in classical mythology by the wife of King Latinus , whose daughter Lavinia married Aeneas and (according to the story in the Aeneid) became the mother of the Roman people.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of the various boys' names with the first syllable Ed-, especially Edward .

variants.name: Edward
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Amye de Gregor was born about 1292, in Nottinghamshire, England, her father, Thomas Gregor, was 40 and her mother, Margaret Chandos, was 19. She married John Annesley in 1308, in Annesley, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in Annesley, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Amye de Gregor est née environ 1292, à Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Gregor, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Margaret Chandos, avait 19 ans. Elle a épousé John Annesley en 1308, à Annesley, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée à Annesley, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Amye de Gregor um 1292 in Nottinghamshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Gregor 40 und ihre Mutter Margaret Chandos 19. Sie hat 1308 in Annesley, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom John Annesley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb in Annesley, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Amye de Gregor è nata il circa 1292 nel Nottinghamshire, England, sua padre, Thomas Gregor, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Margaret Chandos, aveva 19 anni. Lei sposato John Annesley nel 1308, nel Annesley, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel Annesley, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Amye de Gregorが約1292年にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアで生まれた時，父Thomas Gregorは40歳，母Margaret Chandosは19歳であった。1308年にイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアアネスリーでJohn Annesleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はイギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアアネスリーで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Amye de Gregor(이)가 대략 1292년 영국 노틴그함실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Gregor(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Chandos(은)는 19세였다. 그녀는 1308년 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 아네슬리에서 John Annesley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 아네슬리에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Amye de Gregor родилась около 1292 г. в местности ноттингхамшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Thomas Gregor было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Chandos, 19. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Annesley в 1308 г. в местности аннесли, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в местности аннесли, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.
